title: Venni Karumbeswarar Temple
tagline: Venni Karumbeswarar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Venni Karumbeswarar Temple is a Hindu temple located at Koilvenni in Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: Venni Karumbeswarar Temple is a Hindu temple located at Koilvenni in Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is dedicated to Shiva, as the moolavar presiding deity, in his manifestation as Karumbeswarar. His consort, Parvati, is known as Soundaranayagi. The historical name of the place is Tiruvenni.

topPlaces.name: Kovilvenni
topPlaces.description: Kovilvenni or Koilvenni is a town near Needamangalam in the district of Thiruvarur. It is 24 km away from Thanjavur.

topPlaces.name: Vadapathy
topPlaces.description: Vadapathy is a village in the Papanasam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Munnavalkottai
topPlaces.description: Munnavalkottai is a village in the Needamangalam taluk of Thiruvarur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Valangaiman
topPlaces.description: Valangaiman (also spelt as Valangiman) is a town Panchayat in the Valangaiman taluk of Thiruvarur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is the headquarters of Valangaiman Taluk.

topPlaces.name: Valangaiman block
topPlaces.description: Valangaiman block is a revenue block in the Valangaiman taluk of Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 50 panchayat villages.

topPlaces.name: Valangaiman Taluk
topPlaces.description: Valangaiman Taluk is a taluk in Tiruvarur district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Valangaiman.

topPlaces.name: Arunachalesvarar Temple, Ammapettai
topPlaces.description: Arunachalesvarar Temple is a Siva temple located at Ammapettai in Papanasam taluk, Thanjavur district of Tamil Nadu, India, at a distance of 21 km east from Thanjavur.

topPlaces.name: Deepambalpuram
topPlaces.description: Deepambalpuram is a village in the Papanasam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Venni Karumbeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Venni Karumbeswarar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Venni Karumbeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (70 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (72 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Venni Karumbeswarar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Venni Karumbeswarar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Venni Karumbeswarar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Venni Karumbeswarar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Venni Karumbeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kovilvenni, Vadapathy, Munnavalkottai, Valangaiman, Valangaiman block.
